context: Operator (楊董) completed /session/login at 12:14 TST. Elva (no credentials, no logout/login calls) ran path_b_w2a_read_side_verify.py against fresh logged-in session. Steps 0-3 PASS, step 4 (/position) crashed uvicorn worker process.
severity: CRITICAL — gateway worker process dead, KGI session lost with it; operator relogin needed again
do_not_restart_reason: Operator (楊董) just completed login. If Elva restarts 8787 autonomously the credentials need to be re-entered. STOP and let operator decide.

step_by_step.result: 200 OK, kgi_logged_in=true, account_set=false (operator login succeeded; account_set transitions in step 2)
200 OK, account selected and broker bound
200 OK, symbol=2330 oddLot=false, websocket connected and subscribed
ConnectionResetError [WinError 10054] — uvicorn worker process was killed mid-request
step_by_step.diagnosis: Worker did not log the request line. After [kgiTradeCom::OnData] 4310 the process died with no Python traceback in stdout/stderr — strong signature of native SDK (kgisuperpy / pythonnet COM binding) segfault on list_positions() or upstream callback.

log_tail_at_crash: GET /health HTTP/1.1 200 OK (Elva preflight A — standalone)
GET /position HTTP/1.1 200 OK (Elva probe — account_set=false, returned 200 empty)
(no further log lines — worker process killed)
critical_observations: /position returned 200 TWICE before set_account (account_set=false) — handler code path was exercised without crash
Crash happened only AFTER set_account=true + quote/subscribe/tick — i.e. after upstream KGI session became fully active and tick stream was running
[kgiTradeCom::OnData] 4310 was the last log line — this is kgisuperpy SDK push-channel callback; the next /position call competed with active push handler and crashed
No Python traceback in uvicorn stdout — confirms native-code crash, not Python-level exception
Curl to /health post-crash returned exit 7 (connection refused) — confirms uvicorn process gone, not just hung

differential_from_04_23_zombie.04_23_zombie: Old uvicorn alive but KGI WebSocket dead 3 days; /health flag was stale; /position returned 404 (route not in old in-memory app.py)
differential_from_04_23_zombie.this_crash: Fresh uvicorn (Elva 02:29 TST restart, then operator 12:14 TST login); all routes load; /position returned 200 twice without crash; crash triggered by COMBINATION of set_account=true + active tick subscription + /position call

what_did_NOT_happen_by_elva.no_login_attempt: Operator did /session/login; Elva never invoked it
what_did_NOT_happen_by_elva.no_secret_in_log_or_chat: person_id and account masked (<REDACTED:KGI_PERSON_ID> / <REDACTED:KGI_BROKER_ID> broker visible only because operator's own log line emits it; not Elva-printed in chat)
what_did_NOT_happen_by_elva.no_autonomous_restart_after_crash: Elva DID NOT restart 8787 — that would erase the operator login session. Decision deferred to operator.
what_did_NOT_happen_by_elva.no_kgi_session_kill_signal: Elva never sent SIGTERM/SIGKILL to uvicorn process

hypothesis_for_native_crash: Hypothesis A: kgisuperpy list_positions() invokes a Windows COM object (pythonnet binding to KGI ActiveX SDK) that has a thread-safety bug — concurrent push callback + list_positions causes access violation
Hypothesis B: /position handler in app.py calls a SDK method that internally re-enters the message pump while a push event is mid-dispatch → unhandled exception escapes Python boundary
Hypothesis C: The 4310 OnData event itself is fatal regardless of /position; coincidence that crash showed up on /position

Test plan (after operator decides whether to relogin): repro by isolating each — (1) login + show + set + position WITHOUT quote subscribe; (2) login + show + set + quote subscribe + WAIT 60s without /position; (3) full sequence again to check determinism

blast_radius_assessment.production_app_eycvector_com: UNAFFECTED — Railway api/web running independent of local 8787
blast_radius_assessment.openalice_runner_nssm_service: UNAFFECTED — NSSM service is independent process
blast_radius_assessment.operator_kgi_session_remote_state: Likely orphaned at KGI server side; will time out per KGI's session policy. New login will create a new session. Old session does not pose hard line risk (/order/create still 409 NOT_ENABLED_IN_W1 in code, so even if old session were somehow exploitable, no order can flow)
blast_radius_assessment.secrets: ZERO leak — operator inline PowerShell load (Read-Host -AsSecureString), Elva never touched credentials; uvicorn log contains person_id/broker/account but that file is local-only

next_step_decision_belongs_to_operator: Option 1 (recommended): Operator relogin again, then Elva runs a SUBSET — only /position WITHOUT quote subscribe — to test Hypothesis A vs C
Option 2: Operator relogin, then skip /position entirely; verify /trades + /deals work post-tick-subscribe (those use different SDK methods)
Option 3: Park live read-side verify for now, file a Jason backlog item to add try/except + watchdog around all SDK calls in app.py, then retry next operator window
Option 4: STOP W2a entirely; the gateway is too fragile for live read-side without isolation (each SDK call in its own subprocess to contain crashes)
elva_recommendation: Option 1 — relogin + /position-only test (no quote subscribe) — would isolate whether crash is /position-alone or interaction with active push channel. This is a 30-second test and dispositive for the hypothesis. But this is operator's call; Elva will not autonomously relogin or restart.
